definitions.text: Electromagnetic radiation in the wavelength range visible to the human eye (about 400–750 nanometers): visible light.
examples: As you can see, this spacious dining-room gets a lot of light in the mornings.
We lit the fire to get some heat.

definitions.text: To set fire to; to set burning.
To illuminate; to provide light for when it is dark.
examples: I used my torch to light the way home through the woods in the night.

definitions.text: Having light; bright; clear; not dark or obscure.
examples: The room is light when the Sun shines through the window.

definitions.text: Pale or whitish in color; highly luminous and more or less deficient in chroma.
Served with extra milk or cream.
Having little or relatively little actual weight; not heavy; not cumbrous or unwieldy.
examples: a light load ; a lighter backpack after having removed the books ; light weapons

definitions.text: Having little weight as compared with bulk; of little density or specific gravity.
examples: feathers and cork are light ; oil is lighter than water

definitions.text: A stone that is not thrown hard enough.
To unload a ship, or to jettison material to make it lighter
To lighten; to ease of a burden; to take off.

examples: I lit upon a rare book in a second-hand bookseller's.

definitions.text: To stop upon (of eyes or a glance); to notice
